---
description: 'Guidelines for building C# applications'
applyTo: '**/*.*'
---

# Working Instructions

## Memory

Keep a task list in a file called memory.md. This file should be used to keep track of tasks that you have been 
assigned, as well as any important information that you need to remember. Use an encoding that works best for you, 
but make sure that you are able to read the file and understand what the list of current tasks and context is.

In the memory file, make sure to include any information you have collected underway while solving tasks, so you can 
continue if interrupted.

When you are assigned a new task, add it to the task list in memory.md. When you complete a task, 
remove it from the file.

## Lessons Learned

Maintain a human readable file called `lessons_learned.md` where you document any insights, patterns, or best practices 
you discover while working on tasks. This file should be organized in a way that makes it easy to reference and update 
as you learn new information. Include examples and explanations to help others understand the concepts you have learned.
